column1: Which Fabric data store has a SQL analytics endpoint that is read-only vs full DML?
column2: Lakehouse & Eventhouse SQL endpoints are read-only; Warehouse and Fabric SQL database support full T-SQL DML (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/MERGE)

column1: Best data store for near real-time streaming/time-series telemetry?
column2: Eventhouse (KQL DB) — purpose-built, near real-time (seconds)

column1: Which data store auto-mirrors out and is not a stream target?
column2: Fabric SQL database (OLTP engine, full T-SQL DML, low-latency)

column1: Verb heuristic for "wrangle/clean/combine visually" transform tool?
column2: Dataflow Gen2 — low-code, Power Query, 300+ visual transforms, 150+ connectors

column1: Verb heuristic for "custom logic/ML/arbitrary Python" transform tool?
column2: Notebook (PySpark) — unlimited, code-first, TB-scale

column1: Verb heuristic for "aggregate telemetry/rolling window/real time" transform tool?
column2: KQL (Eventhouse) — summarize/extend/parse

column1: Verb heuristic for "upsert/star schema/stored procedure" transform tool?
column2: T-SQL (Warehouse) — full DML/DDL, window functions, CTEs

column1: Can Dataflow Gen2 schedule or orchestrate other Fabric items?
column2: No — Dataflow Gen2 has no native orchestration; only a pipeline schedules/orchestrates other items

column1: What blocks a Dataflow Gen2 from being scheduled unattended?
column2: A required public parameter (public parameters are Preview) — blocks scheduling/manual trigger

column1: When is an Apache Airflow job the right orchestration surface vs a pipeline?
column2: When the team already has Airflow/Python DAG investment (e.g. astronomer-cosmos for dbt) — not a pipeline replacement

column1: Decision funnel order: mirroring vs shortcut vs pipeline copy?
column2: (1) Entire operational DB needs continuous sync → mirroring (2) Data already query-compatible, no transform → shortcut (3) Needs transformation/reshaping → pipeline copy

column1: Metadata mirroring vs database mirroring — data movement?
column2: Metadata mirroring (Unity Catalog, Dremio) moves zero data — catalog only, shortcuts under the hood. Database mirroring fully replicates

column1: Which shortcut caching sources are supported, and what's the file-size cutoff?
column2: GCS/S3/S3-compatible/on-prem gateway only (never ADLS/Blob); files >1 GB never cached; retention 1–28 days

column1: Which shortcut authorization mode uses the item owner's identity instead of the caller's?
column2: Direct Lake over SQL / T-SQL Delegated mode

column1: Does query acceleration on an Eventhouse shortcut unlock materialized views/update policies?
column2: No — acceleration only closes the query-speed gap; MV/update-policy support remains native-table-only

column1: Query acceleration limits on shortcuts?
column2: ≤900 columns; Parquet files >6 GB not cached; no partition-pruning index support

column1: Streaming engine with unbounded transform power (full PySpark/Scala/UDFs/ML)?
column2: Spark Structured Streaming

column1: Which streaming engine is a transform-and-route hub, not a query engine?
column2: Eventstream — the query destination is always a separate item (Lakehouse, Eventhouse)

column1: Copy activity vs Copy job (CDC, Preview) — key difference?
column2: Copy activity = one-time/scheduled batch; Copy job = scheduled/triggered incremental, CDC-based (includes deletes)

column1: Native table vs unaccelerated shortcut vs query-accelerated shortcut (Eventhouse) — MV/update-policy support?
column2: Native table = yes; unaccelerated shortcut = no; query-accelerated shortcut = still no

column1: PySpark syntax for a deterministic "keep latest" dedup?
column2: Window.partitionBy(key).orderBy(ts.desc()) → row_number() → filter(row_num == 1)

column1: T-SQL syntax for a deterministic "keep latest" dedup?
column2: ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY key ORDER BY ts DESC), filter WHERE row_num = 1

column1: KQL syntax for a deterministic "keep latest" dedup?
column2: summarize arg_max(ts, *) by key

column1: ISNULL vs COALESCE — key risk?
column2: ISNULL is 2-arg, types its result from the FIRST argument (can silently truncate); COALESCE is ANSI, N-args, standard type precedence — prefer COALESCE

column1: KQL equivalent of T-SQL MERGE for upserts?
column2: No direct row-level MERGE — use an update policy (transform-on-ingest) or materialized view arg_max dedup

column1: Is MERGE GA in Fabric Warehouse?
column2: Yes — GA, not preview, despite being a known gap in early previews

column1: Fabric Warehouse IDENTITY column limitations?
column2: Preview only, bigint-only, no custom seed/increment, can't ALTER TABLE ADD — ROW_NUMBER() is the GA-safe surrogate-key default

column1: Are global temp tables (##table) supported in Fabric Warehouse?
column2: No — only local #temp tables; distributed WITH (DISTRIBUTION = ROUND_ROBIN) recommended over default non-distributed

column1: COPY INTO supported FILE_TYPE values in Fabric Warehouse?
column2: CSV, JSONL, PARQUET only — no ORC (unlike Synapse dedicated pools)

column1: Are PRIMARY KEY/FOREIGN KEY/UNIQUE constraints enforced in Fabric Warehouse?
column2: No — NOT ENFORCED, optimizer hints only, don't block bad data

column1: broadcast() misuse risk in PySpark joins?
column2: Broadcasting a large table causes OutOfMemoryError, not speedup — only broadcast the small side

column1: Does dropDuplicates() guarantee which duplicate survives?
column2: No — never deterministic; use the window row_number pattern when "which one wins" matters

column1: partitionBy() on write — what does it control?
column2: Physical folder layout for pruning, NOT an in-memory partition count; partition on low-cardinality columns only

column1: Update policy vs materialized view in KQL — problem each solves?
column2: Update policy = transform-on-ingest (any reshaping, per-ingest); materialized view = continuous aggregation only, always GA-fresh

column1: IsTransactional: true on a KQL update policy does what?
column2: Rolls back source ingestion if the transform query fails

column1: KQL lookup vs join — broadcast size assumption?
column2: lookup assumes the LEFT side is the large fact table and broadcasts the RIGHT (small dim) — opposite of join's default (left assumed smaller)

column1: Does KQL have a dedicated hopping or sliding window operator?
column2: No — approximate via bin() variations; a scenario naming a hopping/sliding KQL function is inventing one

column1: Spark watermark behavior on late data?
column2: .withWatermark(col, tolerance) bounds state retention — late events aren't rejected from the stream, just excluded from an already-finalized window

column1: KQL watermark support?
column2: None — bin() queries just re-evaluate against current table contents at query time

column1: Denormalization primitive in KQL for fact/dim enrichment?
column2: lookup kind=leftouter (dim) on key — purpose-built, chainable across multiple dimensions

column1: PySpark denormalization primitive for a small dimension table?
column2: .join(broadcast(dim), ...) — avoids shuffling the large fact table

column1: Which Git providers does Fabric support?
column2: Azure DevOps (cloud), GitHub (cloud), GitHub Enterprise cloud only — no on-prem GitHub Enterprise Server

column1: Who can connect/disconnect a workspace from Git?
column2: Workspace Admin only

column1: Can Commit and Update happen in the same action?
column2: No — sync is one direction per action: Commit (workspace→Git) OR Update (Git→workspace), never both

column1: Which Git conflict-resolution path supports real PR-based code review?
column2: Resolve in Git: checkout new branch → commit → PR/merge on the Git provider → switch workspace back

column1: What happens to unsupported items when a workspace connects to Git?
column2: They're ignored, not deleted — still visible in the panel

column1: Branch out to new workspace vs Switch branch — key difference?
column2: Branch out (Contributor+) = full isolated copy, parent/child shown, uncommitted items lost. Switch branch (Admin default) = overwrites current workspace in place, no uncommitted changes allowed

column1: What does committing a Warehouse/SQL database to Git auto-generate?
column2: A SQL database project (.sqlproj), one .sql file per object — never hand-edit .sqlproj, Fabric overwrites it every commit

column1: What does a database project track — schema, data, or security?
column2: Schema only — no data, no SQL security (roles/RLS/permissions); use pre/post-deployment scripts for static reference data

column1: Known data-loss gotcha in Fabric database projects?
column2: ALTER TABLE ... ADD on a column/constraint drops and recreates the table — workaround: create-new/copy-data/rename

column1: Deployment pipeline stage count — range and mutability?
column2: 2–10 stages, default Dev/Test/Prod; stage count and names are PERMANENT after creation (public stage names are changeable)

column1: What is the tie-breaker for deployment pipeline item pairing?
column2: Folder — only when duplicates of same name+type exist in one stage; a folder mismatch prevents pairing outright

column1: Backward deployment rules in a deployment pipeline?
column2: Only into an EMPTY target stage, full deploy only — never selective

column1: Which item types support deployment rules, and which rule types?
column2: Data source rule: Dataflow Gen1, semantic model, paginated report, mirrored database. Parameter rule: Dataflow Gen1, semantic model. Default lakehouse rule: Notebook only

column1: Does a deployment pipeline ever copy data?
column2: Never — only schema/metadata, plus data sources/parameters, report visuals, model metadata, relationships, sensitivity labels

column1: Spark starter pool vs custom pool — who configures each?
column2: Starter pool: nobody (managed), Medium node only. Custom pool: workspace Admin + capacity toggle "Customized workspace pools", Small–XX-Large

column1: Default and max high-concurrency notebook sessions?
column2: Default 5 notebooks/session, raisable to 50 via spark.highConcurrency.max; only the initiating notebook/activity is billed

column1: Does domain assignment affect data access?
column2: No — domain assignment affects discovery/governance only, never access (that's workspace role + item permission)

column1: Do subdomains have their own domain admins?
column2: No — subdomains always inherit the parent domain's admins

column1: Which OneLake shortcut sources support shortcut caching?
column2: GCS/S3/S3-compatible/on-prem gateway only — never ADLS/Blob; retention 1–28 days, files >1 GB never cached

column1: What is the one July 21, 2026 blueprint wording change, and what did it NOT remove?
column2: Configure Dataflows Gen2 workspace settings → "Configure Apache Airflow workspace settings" — Dataflows Gen2 itself is not removed from the product

column1: Where do Airflow DAG .py files and private packages/wheels go?
column2: DAGs → the "dags" folder. Private packages/wheels → "plugins" (or plugins/libs if no Git connected), then restart the job

column1: Do Free and PPU workspaces support Apache Airflow jobs?
column2: No — Free and PPU workspaces don't support Apache Airflow jobs; private networks/VNets not yet supported

column1: Where to check if a pipeline/notebook/dataflow run succeeded?
column2: Monitor hub

column1: How to resume a pipeline that failed partway through?
column2: Pipeline run history → Rerun from failed activity — resumes only the failed activity and everything downstream; already-succeeded activities are not re-executed

column1: Does Monitor hub cover Dataflow Gen1?
column2: No — Monitor hub covers 17 item types but NOT Dataflow Gen1; a frequent distractor

column1: Monitor hub retention limits?
column2: 100 rows / 30 days on the main view; 30 days per-item — not a durable audit log

column1: Import refresh vs Direct Lake framing — what moves and how long?
column2: Import: entire data volume into VertiPaq cache, minutes–hours. Direct Lake: metadata only (Parquet file references), seconds

column1: Does Direct Lake on OneLake ever fall back to DirectQuery?
column2: Never. Direct Lake on SQL endpoints can, governed by DirectLakeBehavior (Automatic/DirectLakeOnly/DirectQueryOnly)

column1: How to diagnose a DirectQuery fallback?
column2: EVALUATE TABLETRAITS() → DirectLakeFallbackInfo column

column1: What deactivates a semantic model's scheduled refresh automatically?
column2: 4 consecutive credential failures

column1: Scheduled refresh caps per license tier?
column2: Pro = 8/day; PPU and Premium/Fabric capacity = 48/day

column1: Does DELETE /refreshes/{id} cancel a portal-button refresh?
column2: No — only enhanced-API-triggered refreshes, not portal-button or scheduled ones

column1: Activator: stateless vs stateful condition — when does each fire?
column2: Stateless (value < threshold) fires every qualifying event. Stateful (BECOMES/DECREASES/INCREASES/EXIT RANGE/heartbeat) fires only on state transition

column1: Which alert sources are UNSUPPORTED by Activator?
column2: Capacity Metrics app and SQL analytics endpoint directly — route capacity alerts via Real-Time hub's Fabric capacity overview events instead

column1: Activator rate limits: max incoming events/sec/rule and email/item/hour?
column2: 10,000 events/sec/rule; 500 email messages/Activator item/hour (30/rule/recipient/hour)

column1: Workspace-wide pipeline-failure alerting pattern?
column2: Enable workspace monitoring → Activator rule on ItemJobEventLogs — covers every pipeline including future ones

column1: Can the Capacity Metrics app fire alerts?
column2: No — never, under any circumstance; scoped to capacity admins for CU/throttling/storage viewing only

column1: Capacity Metrics app data retention?
column2: 14 days (Compute); 30 days (Storage); dimensions refresh once daily at midnight local time

column1: Does a "successful" pipeline run guarantee a Direct Lake model has reframed?
column2: No — check the semantic model's refresh history Direct Lake tab separately; framing is the freshness mechanism

column1: Dataflow Gen2 refresh history retention?
column2: 50 runs UI / 250 runs or 6 months in OneLake; logs retained 28 days

column1: Where to check which rows failed in a Copy job's table mapping?
column2: Workspace monitoring → CopyJobActivityRunDetailsLogs

column1: OPTIMIZE combined order when bin compaction, Z-Order, and V-Order all apply?
column2: bin compaction → Z-Order → V-Order

column1: V-Order default setting on new workspaces?
column2: Off (spark.sql.parquet.vorder.default = false, writeHeavy profile)

column1: V-Order write cost vs read benefit?
column2: ~15% slower writes; up to 50% more compression, 40–60% faster cold-cache Direct Lake queries, ~10% faster SQL endpoint reads

column1: VACUUM default retention period?
column2: 7 days; shorter requires spark.databricks.delta.retentionDurationCheck.enabled = false

column1: Adaptive target file size range for OPTIMIZE?
column2: 128 MB (tables <10 GB) scaling to 1 GB (tables >10 TB)

column1: Two small-file defenses in Lakehouse — when does each apply?
column2: Optimize write (pre-write bin-packing, before write lands) vs auto compaction (post-write, immediately after commit) — both off by default under some profiles

column1: Partition on low-cardinality columns — what fixes higher-cardinality filters?
column2: Z-Order; over-partitioning recreates the small-file problem one level up

column1: Fabric Warehouse automatic statistics types?
column2: Histogram (_WA_Sys_), avg column length (VARCHAR >100 chars), table cardinality — all synchronous at query time, in sys.stats

column1: Is Fabric Warehouse result-set caching currently live?
column2: No — tenant-wide disabled since 2026-02-16 (known bug); result_cache_hit=0 right now may just mean the feature is off

column1: Fabric Warehouse in-memory + disk caching — can it be disabled?
column2: No — always on, cannot be disabled or manually cleared

column1: Fix for a slow row-by-row INSERT loop in Fabric Warehouse?
column2: Replace with CTAS or set-based INSERT...SELECT

column1: Is AQE on by default in Fabric Spark?
column2: Yes — on by default across all runtimes; a scenario option to "enable AQE" is a distractor

column1: Autotune default state and iteration requirement?
column2: Off by default (spark.ms.autotune.enabled=false), Preview; needs ~20–25 iterations of a repeated query shape; incompatible with high concurrency/private endpoints/Runtime >1.2

column1: Native execution engine (NEE) — I/O-bound vs compute-heavy fit?
column2: Helps compute-heavy Parquet/Delta joins/aggs, not I/O-bound; falls back to JVM automatically and silently on unsupported operators

column1: repartition(n) vs coalesce(n)?
column2: repartition = full shuffle, increase or decrease. coalesce = no full shuffle, decrease only — the fix for post-filter small-file writes

column1: Eventhouse: caching policy vs retention policy — which affects query speed?
column2: Caching policy only — retention policy controls what exists at all (deletes data); shortening retention never speeds up queries

column1: Materialized view vs update policy — cost model in Eventhouse?
column2: Update policy: per-ingested-batch cost, folded into ingest. Materialized view: continuous background CU — best for repeatedly-read aggregates

column1: Eventstream throughput plateau below 4 Event Hubs source partitions — the fix?
column2: Repartition the Event Hub — the throughput-level setting is not the bottleneck

column1: Direct Lake guardrail breach — OneLake variant vs SQL variant?
column2: Direct Lake on OneLake: refresh FAILS, no fallback. Direct Lake on SQL: falls back to DirectQuery (if enabled), succeeds but slower

column1: Workspace-managed staging timeout for Copy jobs into Fabric Warehouse?
column2: 60 minutes — use external storage for longer jobs

column1: Real fix for a ForEach-wrapped per-row pattern taking hours?
column2: Redesign to set-based operation — not batchCount tuning (same lesson as Warehouse's row-by-row INSERT anti-pattern)

column1: Does Viewer get ReadAll (OneLake APIs/Spark) by default?
column2: No — Viewer gets ReadData (TDS/T-SQL) but NOT ReadAll; the single most tested fact in the workspace-role table

column1: Max users/groups per workspace in roles, and multi-group membership rule?
column2: Max 1,000; multi-group membership → highest role wins, no capping

column1: When do workspace role changes take effect?
column2: On the user's next login, not mid-session

column1: Does removing an item-level share revoke access if the user still holds Contributor+ workspace role?
column2: No — item permission and workspace role are independent grants; full revocation requires clearing both layers

column1: What third independent access axis blocks a Contributor from scheduling a gateway refresh?
column2: Gateway permissions — managed outside Fabric roles; Contributor everywhere still can't schedule without a gateway-level permission

column1: Semantic model DAX RLS — who is it enforced for?
column2: Only Viewer; Contributor+ already has blanket view/modify access that supersedes it

column1: Does Warehouse T-SQL RLS exempt db_owner or CONTROL?
column2: No — filter predicates apply to every principal, including dbo/db_owner; no role exemption

column1: Who bypasses Warehouse DDM without an explicit GRANT UNMASK?
column2: Admin/Member/Contributor — they carry implicit CONTROL, which bundles UNMASK

column1: What OneLake permission overrides a OneLake security Read restriction?
column2: Implicit workspace Write (held by Admin/Member/Contributor) — overrides a OneLake security Read restriction

column1: Does Fabric Warehouse RLS support a BLOCK predicate type?
column2: No — Fabric Warehouse RLS supports FILTER predicates only; BLOCK predicates are SQL Server/Azure SQL only

column1: How to modify an RLS predicate function in Fabric Warehouse?
column2: drop policy → alter function → recreate policy — schema-bound policy blocks direct ALTER FUNCTION

column1: CLS: what happens when a user queries a column they lack access to?
column2: A hard permission error, not a silently dropped column

column1: Which items are supported by OneLake security, and which is NOT?
column2: Lakehouse (Read, ReadWrite), Azure Databricks Mirrored Catalog (Read), Mirrored Database (Read). Warehouse is NOT supported — uses T-SQL RLS/CLS/OLS instead

column1: What is DefaultReader in OneLake security?
column2: Auto-created virtual membership = anyone with item ReadAll; must be deleted/restricted to actually narrow access via a custom role

column1: OneLake security table access across multiple roles — union or intersect?
column2: Table access UNIONS across roles (any granting role = access); RLS/CLS intersect within a role, except SQL-endpoint CLS which intersects across ALL a user's roles

column1: Can a ReadWrite OneLake security role carry RLS/CLS?
column2: No — write access is incompatible with hiding rows/columns in the same role

column1: Which shortcut identity mode silently bypasses per-user OneLake security?
column2: Direct Lake over SQL / T-SQL Delegated mode — evaluates using item owner's identity, not the viewer's

column1: The 4 Dynamic Data Masking functions Fabric ships?
column2: default(), email(), random(m,n), partial(prefix,"pad",suffix) — no datetime() (on-prem SQL Server only)

column1: Is DDM encryption?
column2: No — DDM masks are display-only; WHERE clauses still evaluate against real stored values, letting range/equality queries infer masked values

column1: Does sensitivity-label flow support Power BI → Fabric?
column2: No — only Fabric → Fabric and Fabric → Power BI; Power BI → Fabric is unsupported

column1: Which export formats carry a sensitivity label, and which doesn't?
column2: Excel/PDF/PowerPoint export, Analyze in Excel, PivotTable in Excel (E3+), download to .pbix carry it — .csv/.txt export does NOT

column1: Endorsement tiers and their authorization requirement?
column2: Promoted: any item owner/write-permission holder. Certified & Master data: only tenant-admin-authorized reviewers + write permission — write permission alone is never sufficient

column1: Current Fabric/Power BI audit log retention defaults by tier?
column2: 180 days (non-E5) / 1 year (E5) / up to 10 years (retention add-on) — not the outdated 90-day figure

column1: Eventstream delivery guarantee end to end?
column2: At-least-once everywhere — "exactly-once" only ever describes a downstream sink's idempotent-write behavior, never Eventstream's own contract

column1: The 7 no-code Eventstream operators?
column2: Filter, Manage fields, Aggregate, Group by, Union, Expand, Join (plus a SQL operator in preview)

column1: Which 4 Eventstream destinations support a pre-ingestion operator directly?
column2: Lakehouse, Eventhouse (event processing before ingestion), Derived stream, Activator — others (e.g. custom endpoint) need a derived stream hop first

column1: Eventstream max message size and max retention?
column2: 1 MB max message size; 90 days max retention

column1: Recommended minimum capacity for Eventstream?
column2: F4

column1: Spark Structured Streaming output mode requiring no aggregation, and the default?
column2: append (default) — only new rows, universally supported; complete/update both require aggregation and raise an analysis exception without one

column1: Is checkpointLocation mandatory for production Spark Structured Streaming?
column2: Yes — mandatory; tracks offsets + committed batch IDs; one dedicated path per query, never shared

column1: Can MERGE be called directly on a streaming DataFrame?
column2: No — use foreachBatch to run a batch MERGE per micro-batch

column1: Does the Native Execution Engine accelerate Structured Streaming?
column2: Never — streaming always runs on standard JVM Spark regardless of NEE being enabled

column1: .trigger(availableNow=True) behavior?
column2: Bounded catch-up run, then stops

column1: KQL streaming ingestion vs queued (default) ingestion — latency and best use?
column2: Streaming: sub-second to a few seconds, best for small/frequent writes. Queued: seconds–low minutes, best for high-volume throughput/reliability-first

column1: Do query-accelerated Eventhouse shortcuts support materialized views?
column2: No — still no, even GA-accelerated; MV/update-policy require native tables only

column1: Window types Eventstream Group by supports natively?
column2: Tumbling, hopping, sliding, session — all native, no-code

column1: Does KQL have a native hopping window operator?
column2: No — approximate via bin() variants; same for sliding (not a standing construct)

column1: Spark session window function?
column2: session_window(col, "10 minutes")

column1: Eventstream watermark mechanism?
column2: Configurable late-arrival tolerance on Group by — events within tolerance still join an open window

column1: Does a watermark reject late data from the source stream?
column2: No — it only bounds how long window/dedup state is retained; late events just miss an already-finalized window's result

column1: Medallion silver layer role and technology for streams?
column2: Cleansed, deduplicated — Spark watermark+dropDuplicates, or Eventhouse update policy

column1: Default write mode for streaming writes to Delta — where does upsert/aggregation happen?
column2: append is default; upsert/aggregation always happens downstream, never in the streaming write itself

column1: DeltaFlow (preview) purpose, and which CDC sources support it?
column2: Auto-shapes CDC JSON to table columns; supports Azure SQL DB, Azure SQL MI, SQL Server on VM, PostgreSQL CDC only
